case_name: Daphane Fonville, as Parent and Natural Guardian of Derek Fonville, a Minor v. Louay Zeid, M.D. and Usha J. Mehta, M.D., P.A., a Mississippi Corporation

summary.brief: Daphane Fonville, as Parent and Natural Guardian of Derek Fonville, a Minor v. Louay Zeid, M.D. and Usha J. Mehta, M.D., P.A., a Mississippi Corporation. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: On the evening of October 30, 2014, Daphane was admitted to Baptist Memorial Hospital in Southaven, Mississippi, for labor induction after being diagnosed with severe preeclampsia.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Daphane’s labor progressed slowly; when it came time for her to push, she was too tired. At that point, Dr. Zeid informed her that he could either use the vacuum to facilitate delivery, or he could perform a caesarean section.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Derek had a limp arm as well as bruising on his neck and shoulder. He was later diagnosed with a brachial plexus injury, which causes weakness or paralysis in parts of the arm.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In the Defendants’ initial designation of expert witnesses, both doctors indicated reliance on the ACOG monograph, which discusses maternal forces of labor as a potential cause of transient brachial plexus injuries.
evidence_index.speaker: Defense expert

evidence_index.text: The jury returned a verdict for the Defendants. Daphane filed a motion for judgment notwithstanding the verdict or, alternatively, for a new trial. The circuit court denied Daphane’s motion.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: In conclusion, there was no trial by ambush. Daphane was aware of the defense experts’ supplemental opinions and had the opportunity to depose them for a second time.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, this Court affirms the circuit court’s order denying the post-trial motion and entry of judgment for the Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

options.clinical_reasoning: Caesarean section may reduce risk of complications in severe preeclampsia
Chosen due to patient's fatigue and slow labor progression

actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Patient was too tired to push, and vacuum was used to facilitate delivery

expected_action_court.description: Caesarean section should have been considered

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on expert testimony, caesarean section was a safer option given the patient's condition
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Caesarean section should have been performed to avoid complications
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

key_findings: Court affirmed the decision for the defendants

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Cesarean section could have prevented brachial plexus injury
